title: Undertaker's Enchants
description: Adds dozen of new enchantments to Minecraft
body:
Introducing Undertaker's Enchants, a new mod for Minecraft 1.20.1 Forge that adds 18 unique and powerful enchantments to enhance your gameplay.

Key Features:

18 New Enchantments: Discover a variety of powerful enchantments, including:
- Armor Shredder: Weaken your enemies' armor with every strike.
- Berserk: Unleash your inner fury as your health dwindles.
- Critical Damage: Deal devastating critical hits.
- Curse of Bad Health: Pay a price for power with reduced maximum health.
- Curse of Gravity: Become grounded as gravity pulls you down.
- Good Health: Increase your maximum health to survive longer.
- Life Steal: Drain the life from your enemies to heal yourself.
- Magnetic: Gather loot effortlessly with a magnetic pull.
- Sudden Impact: Strike first and strike hard with increased damage on the initial attack.
- Traveller: Move faster and reach new heights with ease.
- Press the Attack: Build up damage with each successive shot.
- Night Vision: See in the dark with ease.
- Bouncy Arrows: Watch your arrows bounce between multiple enemies.
- Extended Reach: Interact with blocks from a greater distance.
- Paradoxical Unbreaking: Makes your tool unbreakable, but for what cost?
- Breath of Svarog: Imbued with the heat of Svarog himself, this enchantment allows tools to melt nearly anything they touch. However, this powerful ability comes at a cost: you cannot apply Fortune or Silk Touch enchantments to the same item.
- Loot Rogue: Increases your chances of receiving double loot from defeated mobs by up to 33%. Get ready to fill your pockets!
- Negation Zone: Creates a zone around the player that nullifies incoming projectiles. While this makes you virtually invulnerable to ranged attacks, it's the only enchantment that can be applied to the item.
 
Undertaker's Enchants is the first modding project by me - undertaker_j (aka JuSTGh0sT1242), a dedicated Minecraft player and creator. I will continue updating this mod, if someone will actually use it.
